No Poverty.reason: The course title and description explicitly focus on 'Poverty and Inequality,' directly addressing its causes and consequences, aligning perfectly with multiple targets of SDG 1.
evidence: Poverty and Inequality in Era of Urbanization
study the causes and consequences of poverty and inequality

Zero Hunger.reason: While poverty is linked to hunger, the syllabus contains no explicit mention of food security, agriculture, or malnutrition, making any connection too indirect for a score.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: Dedicated weekly topics on 'Fertility & Family Planning' and 'Mortality,' with readings on infant mortality and health aid, explicitly align with Targets 3.2 and 3.7.
evidence: Health Aid Reduce Infant and Child Mortality

Quality Education.reason: The course is a form of tertiary education and aims to develop academic skills, but its subject matter does not focus on improving educational systems or access beyond its own offering.
evidence: very good writing samples for future graduate school application

Gender Equality.reason: A specific weekly topic, 'Missing Women & Marriage Market,' with relevant readings, directly addresses gender discrimination and its economic and social consequences, aligning with Targets 5.1 and 5.3.
evidence: the Effect of Sex-Specific Earnings on Sex Imbalance

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: The course explicitly focuses on 'urbanization,' 'slums and homelessness,' and 'upgrading and redevelopment schemes,' which are core to Target 11.1 (slum upgrading) and 11.3 (sustainable urbanization).

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: No evidence found in the syllabus regarding sustainable consumption, production patterns, waste management, or resource efficiency.

Climate Action.reason: No explicit mention of climate change, climate action, or related environmental policies in the syllabus.

Life Below Water.reason: No evidence found in the syllabus regarding marine ecosystems, ocean health, or sustainable fisheries.
